guidebook.hero_title: WELCOME TO THE HOUSTON LEARNING CENTER
items: Emergency: Dial 911 immediately.
Follow posted 10 mph speed limit on campus.
PPE must be worn at all times in lab and bay areas.
Photography is not permitted without authorization.

safety_content.title: LIFE SAVING RULES
safety_content.text: We start work only when it is safe and stop when it's not.

items: Whiteboard: Erased and cleaned.
Projector: Turned off and screen retracted.
Laptops: Closed, unplugged, and stored securely.
Classroom: Tables aligned and chairs pushed in.
Security: Doors locked and lights turned off.

atlas_reference.title: Attendance & Evaluation — ATLAS
atlas_reference.body: Attendance for all internal classes is recorded in ATLAS (Advanced Training Log and Assessment System). ATLAS is the standard system for attendance tracking, student evaluations, and training records in alignment with DOL apprenticeship program requirements.
atlas_reference.note: Attendance must be recorded first thing in the morning — not end of day.

arrival_steps.title: Arrival at the HLC
steps: Check in with the gate guard and park in visitor parking on your first visit.
Proceed to the security desk in the lobby and ask security to contact your PLL.
Your PLL will issue you a blue HLC staff badge and direct you to your classroom or lab area.
If your PLL is unavailable, ask security to contact any PLL or the Training Delivery Manager.
Once checked in, move your vehicle to the staff parking lot on the east side behind Building 1.
If you already have a blue HLC staff badge, proceed directly to staff parking after checking in with the gate guard.
Contact your PLL upon arrival to confirm you are onsite and for further direction.
Your blue HLC staff badge provides 24/7 facility access. If you do not have one, contact your PLL.
HLC business hours: Monday–Friday, 7:30 AM – 4:30 PM.

escalation_matrix.title: Primary Point of Contact for Issues
escalation_matrix.intro: The instructor is the primary point of contact for students during class and should escalate to the applicable contact as needed.

rows.note: Training equipment, simulation, training content, etc.
Student behavior, medical, car accident, passport, etc.
Office supplies, certificates, handouts, manuals, catering, etc.
HVAC, projectors, lights, elevators, internet, housekeeping, etc.

sections.heading: Safety Video
sections.body: The safety video MUST be shown to all incoming students on the first day of class before the start of technical training. The video is approximately 11 minutes. Students who arrive after class starts will be shown the video by security.
sections.note: Verify access to the video prior to your arrival at the HLC. For access issues, contact Jim Rosen or your PLL. HLC IT has USB and DVD copies as backup.

sections.heading: PPE Requirements
items: Safety glasses with side shields must be worn at all times in training bays and lab areas.
Substantial footwear (fully enclosed shoe) must be worn at all times in training bays and lab areas, even within yellow lines.
Safety shoes must always be worn outside the yellow lines.
Hard hats must be worn when overhead work is being performed.
Additional PPE (hearing protection, gloves, additional eye protection) must be worn in designated areas or when performing designated tasks.
Refer to the Safety Risk Assessment (SRA) for the task you are performing.
Do not wear clothing or jewelry that could get caught in machinery.
sections.note: PPE requirements are posted on the door when entering lab areas.

sections.heading: POWRA — Point of Work Risk Assessment
sections.body: A POWRA must be completed before beginning any hands-on work in lab or bay areas. Contact your PLL for the current POWRA form.

locations.name: Building 1 Lobby
locations.description: Common office supplies (dry erase markers, pens, paper, hand sanitizer). PPE items including gloves, safety glasses, shoe covers, hard hats, and masks. Hard hats and overshoes are for temporary use and must be returned. See security at the front desk for access or to request more.

locations.name: Building 1 — Room 1202 (2nd Floor)
locations.description: Office supplies only. Accessible with your employee badge.

locations.name: Classroom Cabinets
locations.description: Each classroom has a black cabinet stocked with notepads, pens, name tags, and attendance sheets. For access issues contact Boyce Hernandez. For restocking contact Chanelle Williams. Do not share passcodes with students or use cabinets for personal storage.

av_system.title: A/V System — Rooms 1233/1235 and 1128–1131 (Building 1)
av_system.intro: These rooms use a Crestron switcher system. Follow the steps below to set up A/V before class.
steps: Power on the projector using the Crestron panel (code: 1234).
Select your input source: DisplayPort (HMI tower), HDMI (laptop — preferred), or VGA (laptop).
For HDMI: Connect HDMI cable to laptop. Sync projector by holding FUNCTION key and toggling to 'Duplicate'.
For VGA: Connect VGA cable to laptop. Select VGA on the Crestron panel.
Verify Crestron Switcher rear connections: VGA, Audio, HDMI, DisplayPort, data cable, and power cable all connected.
Verify Crestron Switcher front panel: PWR light solid green. DM LINK light solid green. No DM LINK light = NO VIDEO.
Verify wall plate: One end of Ethernet cable in black wall plate, other end in DM OUT port on switcher.

facilities_requests.title: Facilities Issues
facilities_requests.body: For any facility-related issues (HVAC, lighting, projectors, elevators, internet, housekeeping), submit a request via the GP Training Operations portal or contact Steve Urban directly.

map.title: CAMPUS MAP & MUSTER POINTS
map.note: See the Site Layout Map below for building locations, muster points, and parking.

emergency.title: FIRE & MEDICAL EMERGENCIES
emergency.instruction: Give your name, location & type of emergency

items: Immediately evacuate via emergency exit routes
Visitors must stay with their host or instructor during an emergency
Know the Emergency Evacuation Routes posted in all classrooms & meeting areas
Dedicated muster points are located in Parking Lots 1, 2 & 3
Do NOT leave the facility or re-enter until ALL CLEAR is announced
Go to tornado shelter locations: Staircases in Bldg. 1 and Bldg. 3
Look for shelter signs on the doors
Follow the posted 10 mph speed limit on all facility roads
PPE is required when performing training in Lab or Bay areas and when working in the facility
Enter the building through the main security entrance
Only trained individuals may operate: fire extinguishers, AED, forklifts, scissor lifts, aerial lifts, and overhead cranes
Report IMMEDIATELY any work-related illnesses, injuries, spills, property damage, or near misses to your host, instructor, or EHS
Taking pictures or photographs is not permitted without site management authorization

signature.label: By entering this facility, you acknowledge and agree to follow all HLC Site Rules.
